mnemonics: Procedural Programs Perform Procedures, Object-Oriented Programs Own Objects (The 4 Ps vs 2 Os)
keyPoints: Procedural Programming focuses on writing procedures or functions that perform operations on data.
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) focuses on creating objects that contain both data and methods.
The four main principles of OOP are Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, and Polymorphism.
commonMistakes: Confusing a Class (the blueprint) with an Object (the actual instance).
Treating Java like a purely procedural language by writing only static methods in a single class.
Forgetting the core OOP principles and writing code that doesn't effectively encapsulate data or use inheritance/polymorphism.
